title: leucanthemum coronopifolium subsp. tenuifolium
intro: Leucanthemum coronopifolium subsp. tenuifolium, also known as the ox-eye daisy, is a plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its white flowers with a yellow center. The medicinal properties of this plant include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with its flowers and leaves having anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum coronopifolium subsp. tenuifolium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antimicrobial activities. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
